item.title: Blackstone-Backed Data Center Firm QTS Kicks Off Bond Sale
item.description: Blackstone Inc.-backed QTS kicked off the sale of a 10-year investment-grade green bond on Monday as the firm builds a major data center in the state of Georgia.

item.title: Senior Housing-Focused REIT National Healthcare Files for US IPO
item.description: National Healthcare Properties Inc., a real estate investment trust, filed for an initial public offering, positioning itself to capitalize on favorable demographic trends associated with a growing elderly population in the US.

item.title: Strategy Posts $14.5 Billion Unrealized Loss in First Quarter
item.description: Bitcoin accumulator Strategy Inc. registered a roughly $14.5 billion unrealized loss in the first quarter as the value of the Michael Saylor-led company’s cryptocurrency holdings fell.

item.title: Israel, Greece Sign €650 Million Deal for Rockets, Launchers
item.description: Israel will supply Greece with launchers and rockets in a deal valued at €650 million ($750 million), the Israeli Ministry of Defense said in a statement.

item.title: US Stock Futures Rise as Dip Buyers Return Amid Ceasefire Hopes
item.description: US equity futures edged higher and crude oil slipped as investors focused on signs of a potential diplomatic push toward a ceasefire in the Iran war.

item.title: Oracle Taps Schneider Electric Executive as New CFO
item.description: Oracle Corp. named Schneider Electric SE executive Hilary Maxson as its new chief financial officer, effective immediately.

item.title: India’s Refiners Delay Maintenance to Stabilize Fuel Supplies
item.description: Indian Oil Corp. and Bharat Petroleum Corp. have delayed routine maintenance at some of their units to ensure adequate supplies in the world’s third-largest oil consumer, according to a senior government official.

item.title: Canadian Confidence Hits 11-Month Low as Iran War Drags On
item.description: Canadian consumer confidence fell to its lowest level in almost a year as the war in Iran, which has sent energy prices soaring and stoked worries about persistent inflation, entered its sixth week.

item.title: Chile Private Debt Attract New Cash Even as US Investors Seek Exits
item.description: SURA Investments and BTG Pactual Chile launched new private-credit funds in Chile, expanding in an industry that has set off alarm bells in the US after years of extraordinary growth.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: Boot Barn, Carvana, Dow, Netflix, Kratos
item.description: US equity-index futures are mixed as traders weigh the latest developments in the war in the Middle East. Contracts on the S&P 500 Index are up 0.1% at 7:40 a.m. in New York.

item.title: Qatar LNG Vessels U-Turn After Attempt to Pass Through Hormuz
item.description: Two tankers carrying liquefied natural gas from Qatar appear to have abandoned an effort to exit the Persian Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz — delaying what would have been the first exports to buyers outside of the region since the war started.

item.title: Israeli Gas Flows to Egypt Return to Pre-War Levels
item.description: Israel’s natural gas flows to Egypt have returned to levels seen prior to the Iran war, according to a person familiar with the matter, after its largest gas field resumed production.

item.title: Italy Is Set to Name New Leonardo Chief as Soon as This Week
item.description: Italy is set to name a replacement for Leonardo SpA Chief Executive Officer Roberto Cingolani as soon as this week, according to people familiar with the matter, capping weeks of negotiations over the key corporate appointment.

item.title: Industrial Firm Madison Air Seeks to Raise $2.23 Billion in IPO
item.description: Madison Air Solutions Corp. is seeking to raise as much as $2.23 billion in its initial public offering, in what would be the biggest US listing of an industrial company in close to three decades.

item.title: India Raises Gas Supply for Fertilizer Firms as Imports Improve
item.description: India has increased the allocation of natural gas to urea plants as supply improves after disruptions due to the Middle East crisis, according to the oil ministry.

item.title: Baseball’s Robo-Umps Aren’t Ready to Replace The Real Umpires Yet
item.description: The automated ball-strike system has often validated the judgement of the human umpires

item.title: NASA Artemis II Astronauts Set to Fly by Far Side of the Moon
item.description: A NASA crew of four astronauts will fly by the moon on Monday at the closest distance they will get to the lunar surface during their mission and the nearest any human has gotten to the moon in more than 50 years.

item.title: UAE’s Surprise $3 Billion Loan Move Puts Pakistan Under Strain
item.description: Pakistan’s economy, already under strain from soaring oil prices, is facing a new setback after it failed to reach agreement with the United Arab Emirates to roll over $3 billion in debt for the first time in seven years.

item.title: Micron Is Slumping and Rival’s $10 Billion US Listing Won’t Help
item.description: Micron Technology Inc. is having a tough few weeks in the stock market, and the upcoming US listing of South Korean rival SK Hynix Inc. will only add to its challenges.

item.title: Buyout Firm ECP to Re-Acquire Nuclear Services Firm EnergySolutions
item.description: Buyout firm Energy Capital Partners has agreed to re-acquire nuclear services firm EnergySolutions from TriArtisan Capital Advisors LLC, according to a statement provided to Bloomberg News.

item.title: Hormuz Traffic Rises to Highest in Weeks
item.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has climbed to its highest levels since the early days of the war, as more countries secure apparent safe-passage agreements with Iran.

item.title: BlackRock Files to Challenge Invesco’s Nasdaq 100 ETF Monopoly
item.description: BlackRock Inc. is setting its sights on a corner of the $13.7 trillion US exchange-traded fund industry long controlled by Invesco Ltd: tracking the Nasdaq 100 Index.

item.title: Oil Shock Is Adding Stress to the Global Economy
item.description: Energy market stress has reached a fever pitch.

item.title: Ex-Wall Street Trader Attempts Distressed M&A Turnarounds in Milei’s Argentina
item.description: After years trading securities in New York for Oppenheimer & Co. and Morgan Stanley, Esteban Nofal’s hunt for returns has brought him home to Argentina.

item.title: OpenAI Advocates Electric Grid, Safety Net Spending for New AI Era
item.description: OpenAI has released a set of policy recommendations meant to help navigate an era of artificial intelligence-fueled upheaval — including suggesting the creation of a public wealth fund, fast-response social safety net programs and speedier electrical grid development.

item.title: Gina Raimondo on How European Industry Is Getting Crushed
item.description: The former Commerce Secretary live at CFR.

item.title: Odd Lots: Raimondo on a Global, Non-China Trading Bloc (Podcast)
item.description: Odd Lots: Raimondo on a Global, Non-China Trading Bloc (Podcast)【原文：Odd Lots: Raimondo on a Global, Non-China Trading Bloc (Podcast)】

item.title: Russia’s Key Black Sea Oil Terminal on Fire, NASA Data Show
item.description: Russia’s key Black Sea oil terminal caught fire following an overnight drone attack, according to satellite images from NASA.

item.title: New Trump Deadline Looms as Ceasefire Push Keeps Markets on Edge
item.description: US allies are reportedly pressing for a last-minute deal with Iran, as President Donald Trump extended his deadline to Tuesday for Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, keeping markets on edge over whether a breakthrough can be reached.

item.title: BOJ Keeps Options Open by Citing Dual Risks in Regional Reports
item.description: The Bank of Japan avoided fueling market expectations over the likelihood of a rate hike this month by keeping its signaling from two quarterly regional economic reports highly nuanced.
